metadata.title: O Holy Night
metadata.subtitle: Cantique de Noël — Adam (1847), English tr. John Sullivan Dwight (1855)
metadata.lyricist: Placide Cappeau (1808–1877, French original); English translation by John Sullivan Dwight (1813–1893)
metadata.x_pd_basis: Adam (d. 1856), Cappeau (d. 1877), and Dwight (d. 1893) all died > 100 years ago

chordpro:
{title: O Holy Night}
{subtitle: Cantique de Noël — Adolphe Adam (1847), English tr. John Sullivan Dwight (1855)}
{key: C}
{time: 12/8}
{tempo: 60}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[C]O holy [G]night, the [Am]stars are brightly [Em]shining
[F]It is the [C]night of our dear [G]Saviour's [C]birth
[C]Long lay the [G]world in sin and [Am]error [Em]pining
[F]Till He ap[C]peared and the [G]soul felt its [C]worth
[Em]A thrill of [B7]hope, the [Em]weary world re[B7]joices
[Em]For yonder [B7]breaks a [Am]new and [D]glorious [G]morn
[C]Fall on your [F]knees [G]
O [Am]hear the [Em]angel [F]voices
[C]O night di[F]vine [G]
O [C]night when [G]Christ was [C]born
[F]O night, [C]O holy [G]night, O [C]night di[G]vine
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[C]Truly He [G]taught us to [Am]love one an[Em]other
[F]His law is [C]love and His [G]gospel is [C]peace
[C]Chains shall He [G]break for the [Am]slave is our [Em]brother
[F]And in His [C]name all op[G]pression shall [C]cease
[Em]Sweet hymns of [B7]joy in [Em]grateful chorus [B7]raise we
[Em]Let all with[B7]in us [Am]praise His [D]holy [G]name
[C]Christ is the [F]Lord [G]
O [Am]praise His [Em]name for[F]ever
[C]His power and [F]glory [G]
ever[C]more pro[G]claim
[F]His power and [C]glory [G]ever[C]more pro[G]claim
{end_of_verse}
